channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Riots, violence, hate: Anti-immigrant unrest spells danger in Belfast
item.description: Crowds exploiting a knife attack are inflicting terror on Northern Ireland's ethnic minority communities.

item.title: New Zealand great Kane Williamson retires from international cricket
item.description: New Zealand's most prolific run-scorer and arguably greatest-ever batsman announces immediate retirement.

item.title: Iraq’s paramilitary groups say they will disarm. Will that ever happen?
item.description: Powerful Shia leader Muqtada al-Sadr among those who say their groups will integrate into the state.

item.title: Family mourn Indian sailor killed in US strike on oil tanker
item.description: The wife and family of an Indian sailor killed in a US strike on a tanker off Oman this week have gathered in mourning.

item.title: ‘Torture isn’t new to Palestinians’: How Israel learned from colonialism
item.description: A chain of sexual violence that Britain built, France refined and Israel inherited is being used against Palestinians.

item.title: USA vs Paraguay: World Cup 2026 group match – Trump, schedule, lineups
item.description: FIFA World Cup cohosts United States begin their campaign against Paraguay, with Donald Trump's attendance in doubt.

item.title: Is Israel’s ‘buffer zone’ inside Lebanon an attempt to grab gas reserves?
item.description: Israeli zone extends into Lebanon's maritime territory, raising fears that it could become a long-term 'resource grab'.

item.title: Video: What Iran’s football fans think about the World Cup
item.description: What’s it like supporting your team in a World Cup hosted by a country that’s at war with yours?

item.title: China arrests US researcher it says is suspected of ‘spying’
item.description: Beijing's Foreign Ministry confirms US reports about detention of scholar focusing on politics of neighbouring Myanmar.

item.title: One in 17 children is working: Here are the industries driving child labour
item.description: On World Day Against Child Labour, Al Jazeera takes a look at the latest numbers on child labour worldwide.

item.title: World Cup 2026 opening day takeaways: Red cards, VAR and hydration breaks
item.description: From criticism over water breaks to red cards galore, here's all the buzz from the opening day of the World Cup.

item.title: Air India crash: Grieving families say justice remains elusive a year later
item.description: A lottery visa that turned into a death warrant. A grieving father fighting deportation, as relatives await reparation.

item.title: Three killed as Ukraine and Russia exchange cross-border attacks
item.description: Officials say two people were killed in Russia's Bryansk, while a woman was killed in Ukraine's Sumy.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire? Not for Indian sailors being killed in Hormuz
item.description: The United States military has struck three vessels in the Strait of Hormuz this week, killing three Indian sailors.

item.title: Video: US defends visa denials at World Cup
item.description: The US has defended visa denials that have prevented officials and fans from travelling to the World Cup.

item.title: Elon Musk poised to make history as world’s first trillionaire
item.description: SpaceX's record-breaking IPO catapults Musk to unprecedented wealth amid rising angst over global inequality.

item.title: Protesters clash with police during World Cup opening match
item.description: Protesters and police clashed outside Mexico City’s Azteca Stadium just before the FIFA World Cup’s opening match.

item.title: Pope Leo’s visit lays bare Spain’s tangled politics of faith and migration
item.description: The American pope's message underlines the rift between his Catholicism and that of Spain's far right.

item.title: Mountain of war: The India-Pakistan conflict’s deadliest battle zone
item.description: After their war last May, India and Pakistan reached a truce. But deaths continued on the world's highest battlefield.

item.title: World Cup day 2: USA, Canada begin – schedule, predictions and how to watch
item.description: North America's two other cohosts take centre stage on Friday as Canada and the US begin their FIFA World Cup campaigns.

item.title: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies at 47 after three years in coma
item.description: The princess, who was known for her legal work, died in a Bangkok hospital after years of illness.

item.title: Iran war day 105: Trump halts attacks after Kharg Island threat
item.description: Trump cancels planned Iran attacks, saying talks are close to a result as Tehran reviews a proposed US deal.

item.title: South Korea rally to beat Czechia 2-1 on World Cup opening day
item.description: Second-half goals by Oh Hyeon-gyu and Hwang In-beom steered South Korea to a win over Czechia in a Group A encounter.

item.title: Peruvian police dress as World Cup mascots to fool suspected drug dealer
item.description: Police officers in Peru dressed up as 2026 World Cup mascots as they targeted a suspected drug trafficker.

item.title: Iran weighs proposed deal but remains wary of US intentions
item.description: Iran says a proposed agreement is under review by its highest leadership and could be closer than ever before.
